content.full_text:
Re
Farn PolrrrcAl- PRACTTcES CoulrrrssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-56@ o Fax (916) 322-0886
May 26,20M
Hiley Wallis, Elections Division Manager
Tulare County Registrar of Voters
221 South Mooney Blvd., Room G-28
Visalia, CA93297
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our FiIe No. I-04-115
Dear Ms. Wallis:
This letter is in response to your request on behalf the County of Tulare Registrar
of Voters for advice regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act').1 Since you are not requesting advicaon the duties of a specific public official,
we are treating your request as one for informal assistance pursrmnt to regulation
18329(c\.2
QUESTION
The County of Tulare is holding a special recall election on August 3,2004, for
the office of governing board member for the Farmersville Unified School District. May
candidates and committees involved in the special election combine the serni-annual
statement due July 31,2004, with the second pre-election statement due July 22,2004?
CONCLUSION
In connection with an August 3 special election, candidates and committees
involved in the election are roquired to file a first pre-election campaign statement no
later than Jwe 24,2004, covering the period through June 19, 2004, and a second pre-
election campaign statement no later than July 22,2004, covering the period June 20
through July 17,2004. Candidates and committees that file the pre-election statements
will satisfu the requirement to file the semi-annual statement due on July 3l ,2004,for
the period through June 30, 2004.
I Govemment Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not prouid" the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Government Code section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. section 18329(c)(3), copy
enclosed.)
eE


File No. I-04-115
Page No. 2
AIYALYSIS
During an election, candidates and committees involved in the election are
required to file two campaign statements disclosing contributions received and
expenditures made in connection with that election. Thesepre-election carnpaign
statements are generally filed 40 days before the election and again 12 dap before the
election- (Section 84200.8-) Semi-annual statememts are filed January 3l and July 31,
respectively. (Secfion 842W.)
There is a mechanism in the Act which permits the oombining of statements when
an overlapping reporting p€riod occurs, or the filing of the semi-annual statement would
be an additional, unnecessary filing to the two pre-election staternents. (Section S4ZO5.)
In this case, filing the two pre-election staternemts in connection with the August 3 special
recall election will satisfu the July 3l,2M,semi-annual filingrequirernemt-
A campaign statement filing schedule which outlines the due dates for filing
statements is enclosed for your oonvenience.
If I may be of any further assistance, please do not hesitate to contact me at (866)
275-3772.
Sincerely,
LuisaMenchaca
General Counsel

By: Carla Wardlow
